conflicts.description: The prior sheet treated the story as roughly four months with a chapter_18 coda several months later, but the manuscript's internal chronology compresses the action into March-through-May and then only weeks of early-rain aftermath.
conflicts.canonical_value: About ten to twelve weeks from late March through May and into the first habitual rains; chapter_18 occurs weeks later.
conflicts.resolution_note: Multiple chapters support a short spring chronology, and chapter_18 explicitly says 'Weeks later,' so the manuscript overrides the older broader estimate.

conflicts.description: Chapter_05 shows weathered intake lettering reading ANNEX C, while chapter_12's recovered logbook and badge identify the wet-system access as Annex B.
conflicts.canonical_value: Use a generic BrightCurrent intake route under camp; the recovered wet-system records and badge are specifically Annex B.
conflicts.resolution_note: The weathered entrance sign is less reliable than the logbook title and access badge found inside the system.

conflicts.description: Chapter_07 dates Beto's wrist burn to last winter, while chapter_15 calls it a fryer burn from May.
conflicts.canonical_value: Beto has a visible kitchen-burn scar on one wrist; the exact month is left unspecified.
conflicts.resolution_note: The visible scar matters for continuity; neither chapter depends causally on the exact burn date, so the date is omitted.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet left Mina's ending role vague, but chapter_18 explicitly puts her back on paper as utility and restricted guide after the chapter_16 firing.
conflicts.canonical_value: After the chapter_16 firing, Mina is formally rehired in chapter_18 as utility and restricted guide and housed in the former linen closet.
conflicts.resolution_note: The coda gives the exact job title, restrictions, and housing arrangement, so the sheet should use that explicit end-state.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet treated Mina's pay as fully lost after chapter_16 except for informal later work, but chapter_18 restores formal wages on a lower-paid schedule.
conflicts.canonical_value: Mina loses wages in chapter_16, then receives lower but regular formal pay in chapter_18 when bookings hold.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript explicitly states that Mina is back on paper with wages tied to bookings, so the financial entry is updated to formal employment rather than informal work.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet left Tomas as a later-suspended county pilot, but the coda shows him with the county patch torn off and no county payroll.
conflicts.canonical_value: Tomas is suspended in chapter_11 and, by chapter_18, is off county payroll and doing repair labor around camp.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter_11 only establishes an uncertain suspension horizon; chapter_18 provides the explicit later employment state and therefore controls canon.
